zh.Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾馬丁
zh.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden於1214年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾馬丁。他於1240年之前與Beatrix Fraser在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1269年在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾馬丁。

zh-hans.Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland: 苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾馬丁
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden于1214年出生在苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾馬丁。他于1240年之前与Beatrix Fraser在苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1269年在他的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾馬丁。

nameMeanings.name: Hadden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Hadden, Halden, or Haulden (Roxburghshire), or a variant of   Haldane  .
Irish (Armagh and Tyrone): though mainly Scottish, this surname is sometimes used as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉidín ‘descendant of Éidín’ (see Hayden ).

variants.name: Haldane
meanings: Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Haldan (Old Norse Halfdanr, Half(f)dan, Anglo-Scandinavian Healfdene, literally ‘half Dane’).
Scottish: shortened form of the Lanarkshire surname McCalden, probably from Mac Haldan ‘son of Haldan’, with the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Hayden
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉideáin ‘descendant of Éideán’ and Ó hÉidín ‘descendant of Éidín’, personal names apparently from a diminutive of éideadh ‘clothes, armor’. There was also a Norman family bearing the English name (see 2 below), living in County Wexford. Alternative spellings include Hadden .

English: habitational name from any of various places called Haydon (Dorset, Wiltshire, Somerset, Gloucestershire), Heydon (Cambridgeshire, Norfolk), or Hayden (Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire). Most of the placenames derive from Old English hēg ‘hay’ or (ge)hæg ‘fence, enclosure’ + dūn ‘hill’, though the Cambridgeshire placename has Old English denu ‘valley’ as the final element.

variants.name: Haddon
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Derbyshire, Northamptonshire, Huntingdonshire, and Devon, named with Old English hǣth ‘heathland, heather’ + dūn ‘hill’, or from Haddon in Cambridgeshire, which is probably named from the Old English personal name Headda + dūn.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden was born in 1214, in Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland. He married Beatrix Fraser before 1240, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1269, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden est né en 1214, à Kilmartin, Argyll, Écosse. Il a épousé Beatrix Fraser avant 1240, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1269, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Kilmartin, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden wurde 1214 in Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland geboren. Er hat vor 1240 in Argyll, Scotland Beatrix Fraser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1269 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden è nato il nel 1214 nel Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland. Lui sposato Beatrix Fraser prima del 1240, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1269, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland: スコットランドアルグイルキルマルティン
ja.Argyll, Scotland: スコットランドアルグイル
ja.lifeSummary: Reverend James Haddenは1214年にスコットランドアルグイルキルマルティンで生まれた。1240年以前にスコットランドアルグイルでBeatrix Fraserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1269年に故郷で55歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルキルマルティンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden(은)는 1214년 스컷랜드 아그이 킬마틴에서 태어났다. 그는 1240년 이전 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Beatrix Fraser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1269년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬마틴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden nasceu em 1214, em Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland. Ele casou-se com Beatrix Fraser antes 1240, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1269, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden родился в 1214 г. в местности килмартинь, аргилл, Шотландия. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrix Fraser перед 1240 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1269 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности килмартинь, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Reverend James Hadden nació en 1214, en Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland. Se casó con Beatrix Fraser antes de 1240, en Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1269, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Kilmartin, Argyll, Scotland.
